action.reasoning: Observation: Only ~5 minutes of total time remain. Best test score so far is 0.0089 from the fully fine-tuned merged model. The base-model evaluation in iteration 21 scored 0.0 with 1092 missing/invalid predictions because the batched predict.py was broken. In isolation the base model with system prompt outputs attribute-style coordinates on val, so a robust single-sample parser could unlock strong zero-shot ScreenSpot-Pro performance. Analysis: The fine-tuned model is proven to suffer catastrophic forgetting on test; even a perfect parser cannot overcome the domain shift. The base model is the only candidate with upside potential. Fixing predict.py to single-sample inference eliminates the batching bug that caused most invalid outputs. Decision: Overwrite predict.py with a reliable single-sample version and submit the offline base model for the final evaluation.
